relationship_disposition.external_term: NIST AI RMF governance and AI risk-management language
relationship_disposition.notes: Accepted only as an overlapping term for Governance Boundary. Equivalent status is not accepted.
Accepted only as an adjacent term. Trustworthiness considerations may inform governance design but do not define StegVerse receipt-bound standing or commit-time admissibility.

accepted_text: NIST AI RMF governance and AI risk-management language, where it addresses structured governance of AI systems and responsible management of AI-related risk. This overlaps with Governance Boundary but is not equivalent because Governance Boundary specifically separates publication, vocabulary, continuity, proof, and commit-time admissibility authority within the StegVerse transition-governance model.
rejected_text: NIST AI RMF governance language is equivalent to Governance Boundary.
decision_basis: NIST AI RMF is a primary government reference for AI risk management and trustworthiness considerations.
The proposal identifies overlap with structured AI governance and risk-management responsibilities.

The proposal also identifies mismatches: NIST AI RMF does not encode Site/Wiki/Continuity/StegCore role separation, does not mint a StegVerse decision receipt, and does not by itself determine commit-time admissibility.
The accepted disposition is overlap only. Equivalent status is rejected.
non_claims: ALLOW_AS_OVERLAP does not accept NIST AI RMF as equivalent to Governance Boundary.
ALLOW_AS_OVERLAP does not claim NIST endorsement of StegVerse terminology.
ALLOW_AS_OVERLAP does not make NIST AI RMF a StegVerse execution-authority boundary.
ALLOW_AS_OVERLAP does not create admissibility standing.
